summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of homepage imagery implementation. Successfully verified all major features including hacker image with 'Active Threat' overlay, SOC analyst monitoring image, stat cards, 404 page functionality, and favicon. Most imagery and overlays are working correctly with minor visibility issues on some images.

ui_bugs.issue: Risk image (stressed person) element exists but is not visible (marked as hidden in DOM)
ui_bugs.note: Image element is present and text overlay works, but image visibility is false - likely CSS or lazy loading issue

ui_bugs.issue: Padlock image was initially hidden but became visible after scrolling
ui_bugs.note: Image loads correctly after page scroll, may be related to lazy loading implementation

Homepage stats section shows 'The Reality' card about 194 days breach detection
Our Approach section shows SOC analyst image with '24/7 Monitoring' overlay
All stat cards (78%, 75%, $4.9M, #1) render correctly with proper styling
Why Us section shows tablet image (visible)
Why Us section shows ransomware warning image with 'This is what a ransomware attack looks like' text
Risk Reversal section shows 'Don't let this be you' overlay text

404 page renders correctly at /nonexistent with all required links (Back to Home, Service Areas, Resources)
CTA button 'Get Your Free Security Audit' is present and functional
All sections have proper data-testid attributes for testing

action_item_for_main_agent: Minor CSS/lazy loading optimization needed: Risk Reversal image visibility issue (element exists but marked as hidden). Consider reviewing image loading strategy for better initial visibility.
